one_line: Alphabet's AI re-rating is late-cycle: +106% YoY, Dow inclusion on June 29 (classic institutionalization signal), $84.75B dilutive equity raise at peak, stock already down 11% from post-Dow high — momentum is breaking before AI monetization has proven out.
catalysts: Bear: DOJ/35-state appeal seeking structural remedies (AdX divestiture ruling pending 2026), monetization lag vs OpenAI/Anthropic (Gemini revenue ramp slower despite 99% smartphone distribution), $84.75B equity dilution, crowded 64-analyst 'Strong Buy' consensus. Bull: Google Cloud +63% Q1 YoY, 1B+ AI Mode monthly users, $462B cloud backlog, Gemma 4 50M downloads in weeks, GM/Volvo/GenAI.mil vertical lock-in. Macro wildcard: hawkish Warsh Fed + Thursday jobs report compresses growth multiples further.

citations.fact: Alphabet officially announced to replace Verizon in Dow Jones Industrial Average effective June 29, 2026 — 54th composition change in the Dow's history.

GOOG at $345.29 on Dow debut day; Google Cloud posted 63% Q1 sales expansion after AI integration; Motley Fool Stock Advisor team did NOT include Alphabet among their 10 best stocks despite Dow inclusion, signaling persistent institutional skepticism.

Gemini revenue ramp is slower than both OpenAI and Anthropic despite Gemini available on 99% of smartphones; Alphabet 'controls the mills, the rail lines, and the distribution network but has not yet proven it can price the steel at a margin that rewards the capital deployed.'
DOJ and 35 state attorneys general appealing Judge Mehta's search antitrust remedy seeking tougher structural remedies; AdX divestiture ruling pending 2026 — dual antitrust overhang unresolved.
